name: Ravioli
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 pounds lean beef (flank or bottom round), diced
1 cup plum tomatoes, peeled, chopped and sieved
1/2 meat from sauce recipe, see above
recipeInstructions:
Sauce for Ravioli
1. Chop rosemary and garlic together.
2. Combine olive oil, butter, and salt pork in a saucepan. Heat.
3. Add onions and sauté slowly until medium brown.
4. Add beef and pork; simmer 20 minutes.
5. Add bay leaves, allspice, black pepper, and the garlic/rosemary mix. Cook for 10 minutes.
6. Add wine, stir, cover, and cook for 3 minutes.
7. Add all tomatoes, boiling water, and salt; simmer for 20 minutes. Remove from heat.
8. Remove all meat from sauce and put through a food chopper. Place half of the meat back in the sauce and stir. Bring to a slow boil. Remove from heat.
9. Put remaining chopped meat aside for the stuffing.
Stuffing for Ravioli
1. Wash spinach and cook until tender. Drain and chop fine.
2. Mix with meat, cheese, eggs, pepper, and nutmeg. Add salt to taste. Mix well.
Homemade Dough for Ravioli
1. Place flour on a long pastry or baker's board.
2. Make a well in the center and drop eggs into it.
3. Gradually add water (4-5 tablespoons) and salt. Blend together and knead until smooth and elastic.
4. Cover and let stand for 30 minutes.
5. Divide into four parts. Roll out the dough, wrapping it around the rolling pin until very thin, as thin as possible.
6. Spread on a flat surface.
Assembling Ravioli
1. Make a row of little mounds of stuffing on the rolled-out ravioli dough using about 1 teaspoon for each. The mounds should be 2 inches from the edge of the dough and 1 1/2 inches apart.
2. When the row is complete, cover by folding the dough over top. Press firmly between the mounds of stuffing with the side of your hand.
3. Cut along the full length of the strip and in between the mounds using a pastry wheel cutter, bearing down on the wheel.
4. Separate and allow to dry for at least 40 minutes. Continue until all dough and stuffing are used.
Cooking Ravioli
1. Place ravioli in boiling salted water and cook for 12 minutes.
2. Drain in a strainer, handling carefully, and place in a warm bowl.
3. Spoon a little sauce over the ravioli and sprinkle with grated Parmesan.
4. Mix gently. Serves 6-8. Refrigerate for the next day or freeze for future use.
